Patch Set 3:

(5 comments)

Patch Set 3:

> What is the overhead of repeatedly opening and closing the changelog every second?

I guess open/close themselves aren't a big problem, the problem is that we rescan same records again and again - the records can persist for quite a while given we preserve them till replication and inability to cancel speific records.

> Maybe I'm missing something, but why not use poll() on the changelog fd to see if there are new records?
the caller does use poll() to check for new records.

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

Alex, can you create an LU ticket to reference in the commit message?

parents.subject: LU-12405 lnet: Oracle OFED extensions default to on

commit.subject: LU-12553  mdc: polling mode for changelog reader
commit.message:
LU-12553  mdc: polling mode for changelog reader

this allows the user (like lsom_sync and similar) to follow
the changelog and don't rescan getting duplicates.

Change-Id: I78dc163838c1b88f9447a4731ad4bfe00fec7eff
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/35262
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
